status: operator-review required; draft only; no upload action

claim_boundary.purpose: Local deposit-metadata planning aid only; not trusted-list validation, not source-signature validation, not supervision, and not public alerting.
does_not_do: upload or transmit repository, dashboard, artifact, or manuscript files
validate source signatures for relying-party purposes
determine provider or service legal status

metadata.title: Cassandra: From Governance Infrastructure to Evidence Infrastructure
metadata.license_planning_note: To be chosen by operator before deposit. Default planning option: CC BY 4.0 for text only if rights review allows it; otherwise leave rights unspecified until cleared.
metadata.description: Cassandra is a bounded case-study observatory for public LOTL-derived trusted-list artifacts. It records dated collection attempts, normalizes comparable XML artifacts, emits aggregate structural diff classes, packages evidence objects, exposes a caveated dashboard index, and records EATF/AEP receipt metadata. The core contribution is methodological: Cassandra shows how public governance artifacts can become reproducible evidence infrastructure while preserving the boundary between evidence integrity and legal interpretation.
metadata.abstract: Public-key infrastructure is governance infrastructure as well as cryptographic substrate: certificates, policies, revocation channels, trusted lists, audits, supervisory conventions, and software distribution coordinate digital trust. Cassandra studies one public surface of that infrastructure: European list-of-lists-derived trusted-list artifacts. The observatory records dated collection attempts, normalizes comparable XML artifacts, emits structural diff classes, packages local evidence bundles, exposes aggregate dashboard data, and attaches EATF/AEP receipt metadata to evidence packages. The case study demonstrates a movement from governance infrastructure to evidence infrastructure: public legal-technical artifacts can be observed, hashed, bundled, displayed, and independently checked while preserving the distinction between evidence integrity and legal interpretation. Cassandra is not a trusted-list validator, not a relying-party source-signature validator, not a supervisory tool, and not a public alerting system. It is a bounded methodological case study for reproducible public-governance-artifact observation and for adjacent AI-governance evidence discussions.

pre_deposit_checklist: Operator chooses target venue or repository and confirms that deposit metadata is wanted.
Operator confirms license, rights, and any employment/IP/confidentiality constraints.
Live repository URL, dashboard URL, and public index are rechecked on the actual deposit date.
Manuscript PDF/DOCX/Markdown are regenerated after any edits and revalidated.
notes/preprint-review-packet-2026-05-27.md blockers are reviewed and either resolved or explicitly accepted as limitations.
References are restricted to notes/cassandra-checked-reference-ledger-2026-05-27.md or newly checked ledger entries.
No provider-specific trusted-list prose is introduced without separate approval.
Abstract and keywords are adjusted to match the final target's metadata fields.
External identifiers and adjacent-project status labels are rechecked immediately before use.
A final no-secrets/no-private-data/no-overclaim scan is run before any manual upload.
future_agent_rule: Do not use this JSON file as permission to deposit. Safe autonomous action is editing and validating local drafts only; upload, DOI reservation, public release note, or venue routing requires explicit operator approval.
